beer.beer_description:
Introducing the next edition of the Hinterland series, Big Pale Ale.
Since day one, Stone & Wood has walked in step with Australian hop farmers. Their dedication and innovation have led to some of the world’s most outstanding and flavourful beers. To shine a spotlight on this amazing produce, we present the all-Australian hop series.
The second release in the series, Hinterland Big Pale Ale has a low malt profile that allows the Eclipse and Vic Secret hops to shine through. It’s citrusy, juicy, and lightly tropical. Too good not to be shared with friends.

items.badge_description: Thanks for enjoying a brew at Stone & Wood Brewery Byron Bay! Keep checking in here to level up all the way to 100. Cheers!

At a new place and don't want to choose a pint of something you may not like? Grab a few tasters! Enjoy some small pours and then decide which to upgrade to a full pour. That's 195 checkins with the serving style of Taster. Try 5 more for Level 40! Want to learn more about this badge? Check out our podcast

Everyone loves fresh beer, but obviously not as much as you do. You went straight to the source. 

That's a beer at 20 different venues categorized as a Brewery, Cidery or Meadery. Visit 5 more for Level 5!

user.bio: I'm a hoptimist, I believe the world is a better place with beer. ?

items.badge_description: Fresh from the tap is one of the best ways to enjoy a beer. Just try to use a glass and not drink directly from the tap, as tempting as it may be. That's 45 checkins with the serving style of Draft. Try 5 more for Level 10!

items.badge_description: It takes the perfect amount of light, the right framing and a touch of creative love to get the perfect picture of your brew. Now it’s time to drink it! That's 410 check-ins with a photo.  Try 5 more for Level 83!

Looking for more kick than a session beer, but want to be able to stay for a few rounds? You have to keep it in the middle. That's 60 beers with an ABV greater than 5% and less than 10%. Try 5 more for Level 13!

items.checkin_comment: Big pale ale, is a big yes…
